text.title: Tools, Services, Agent Workflow, and Gotchas
text.summary: CLI tools for Firefly integration pipelines, agent-accessible services, and critical failure modes to avoid.

list.item: Adobe Firefly Services CLI — batch generation via enterprise API; developer.adobe.com/firefly-services
adobe-io-cli — Adobe I/O API gateway management; npm install -g @adobe/aio-cli
imagemagick — post-processing: resize, format convert, color analysis; imagemagick.org
exiftool — read/write image metadata including AI generation provenance; exiftool.org
sharp (Node.js) — fast image processing for batch pipeline; npm install sharp
colormath (Python) — validate generated image color palette against brand colors; pip install colormath
Adobe Firefly Services (SaaS enterprise) — batch generation REST API; requires enterprise agreement + beta access; agent-friendly
Adobe Creative Cloud API (Photoshop API) — can trigger Generative Fill programmatically; partial agent support
Cloudinary (SaaS) — asset storage + transformation pipeline; integrates with Firefly output; REST API
Brandfolder (SaaS) — digital asset management; stores and distributes Firefly-generated assets; REST API
DALL-E 3 / OpenAI (SaaS) — alternative with strong API for teams without Firefly enterprise access; no commercial-safe training guarantee at Firefly level

section.title: Agent Workflow

rule.statement: Agents operate in two layers of Firefly integration: (1) design-brief generation — produce structured prompts from brand guidelines and content briefs; (2) post-processing review — analyze generated images for brand consistency (color, composition, subject matter). Direct generation requires enterprise Firefly Services API access.
rule.rationale: These two layers are the highest-value agentic tasks in a Firefly workflow. Brief generation improves prompt quality at scale; post-processing review catches non-compliant assets before they reach production.

section.reference: Agent prompt: generate structured Firefly prompts from content brief and brand guide
Agent prompt: audit generated images against brand standards

antipattern.description: Agent assumes a public Firefly API exists and attempts direct generation calls without enterprise access
antipattern.reason: There is no public Firefly API for individuals. Attempts to call it will fail or use unofficial workarounds. Agents must check for enterprise access before attempting API automation.

antipattern.description: Agent runs a batch job requesting many variants per asset without checking available credit balance
antipattern.reason: Credits are consumed per variation. A large batch job can exhaust monthly allowance. Always validate credit balance and estimate consumption before scheduling batch generation.

antipattern.description: Agent treats identical prompts as deterministic and stores only one variant
antipattern.reason: Firefly output is non-deterministic. Store all generated variants with their prompts; human-select before publishing. Never assume the first generated output is production-ready.

antipattern.description: Agent interprets silent rejection (Firefly content moderation error) as a network error and retries without modifying the prompt
antipattern.reason: Firefly silently rejects certain subjects without explanation. Agents must detect rejection-type errors, rephrase the prompt (remove triggering elements), and retry with modified content.

antipattern.description: Agent validates generated assets against brand color by visual description alone
antipattern.reason: Color descriptions in prompts are interpreted loosely. Post-processing color validation requires programmatic comparison (colormath or ImageMagick) against brand palette values, not visual description matching.
